concerns.analysis: The first page of the source PDF features the prominent State Seal of North Carolina at the top. In the HTML output, this image is missing entirely; it is replaced by the text string '[Image: State Seal of North Carolina]' rather than an actual element. The image manifest also confirms this image has a b64_len of 0. Missing the primary government seal on the first page is a critical defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: State Seal of North Carolina graphic at the top center of page 1.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (9): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=9, pages_affected=9/10. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: On Page 10, the 'Cooperators' table suffers from text truncation in the output due to bounding box/OCR crop errors. Specifically, 'Jordan Lake' is extracted as 'h Lake', and 'Mountain Island Reservoir' is extracted as 'untain Island Reservoir'. This alters proper nouns, changing the meaning and obscuring the referenced location.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Jordan Lake - Folkners Branch
...
Mountain Island Reservoir

concerns.analysis: The primary data table spanning pages 4 through 10 of the source PDF has a 7-column structure, with columns 5 and 6 containing monetary values (e.g., '$ 800.00'). While the table is correctly rendered with 7 columns on page 4, the JSON extraction for pages 6, 8, and 9 incorrectly splits the dollar signs into their own distinct columns. This effectively forces the table into a 9-column structure in the output, which breaks the column mapping and misaligns the data beneath the 7-column headers. Fo
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A 7-column table where the 5th and 6th columns contain the dollar amounts in single cells, such as '$ 800.00'.
concerns.output_quote: The data is split across 9 columns, separating the dollar sign: {"text": "$", "column_start": 4}, {"text": "800.00", "column_start": 5}, {"text": "$", "column_start": 6}, {"text": "800.00", "column_start": 7}.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['mailto:Kevin.Hart@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
